question_text: Sound waves are described as mechanical waves. Which of the following best explains what this means?

options.label: A
options.text: Sound waves can travel through a vacuum

options.label: B
options.text: Sound waves require a medium such as air, water, or solid material to travel through

options.label: C
options.text: Sound waves are produced only by machines and engines

options.label: D
options.text: Sound waves travel at the speed of light

correct_answer: Sound waves require a medium such as air, water, or solid material to travel through
explanation: Mechanical waves are waves that need a medium (like air, water, or solids) to travel through because they work by making particles vibrate and pass the wave energy along. Sound is a mechanical wave, so it cannot travel through empty space (a vacuum) where there are no particles to vibrate.
